draft.introduction: Habakkuk’s oracle contains the phrase “the righteous shall live by his faith,” which Paul quotes in Romans 1:17 and Galatians 3:11. This single verse became the engine of the Protestant Reformation.

translation.text: And the Lord answered me: “Write the vision; make it plain on tablets, so he may run who reads it. For still the vision awaits its appointed time; it hastens to the end—it will not lie. If it seems slow, wait for it; it will surely come. Behold, his soul is puffed up; it is not upright within him, but the righteous shall live by his faith.”
translation.original_text: ὁ δὲ δίκαιος ἐκ πίστεώς μου ζήσεται

translator_notes.note: The Hebrew reads “by his faith/faithfulness” (emunah), while the LXX reads ἐκ πίστεώς μου (“by my faithfulness”)—God’s faithfulness rather than human faith. Paul’s quotation in Romans 1:17 drops the pronoun entirely, creating deliberate ambiguity.
